title: What Makes a Shape a Polygon?

steps.work: A polygon is a closed, flat shape made of straight sides.
steps.explanation: Three rules: (1) closed (no gaps), (2) flat (2D), (3) all sides straight (no curves).

steps.work: Closed? Yes. Flat? Yes. Straight sides? Yes. A triangle IS a polygon.
steps.explanation: A triangle passes all three tests.

steps.work: Closed? Yes. Flat? Yes. Straight sides? NO — it is curved. A circle is NOT a polygon.
steps.explanation: The circle fails the straight-sides test.

title: Naming Polygons by Number of Sides

steps.work: Polygons are named by their number of sides using Greek prefixes: tri (3), quad (4), penta (5), hexa (6), hepta (7), octa (8).
steps.explanation: The prefix tells you the number of sides.

steps.instruction: A shape has 8 straight sides and is closed. Name it.
steps.explanation: Count the sides, then use the prefix for that number.

steps.instruction: Confirm this shape is a polygon.
steps.work: 8 straight sides, closed shape, flat → yes, it is a polygon.
steps.explanation: Always check: straight sides, closed, and flat.
The pattern is consistent: match the number of sides to its Greek prefix.
